name: screen
description: GNU Screen terminal multiplexer - manage multiple terminal sessions within a single window

args.name: session_name
args.description: Name for the new screen session

options.name: -d
options.description: Detach the session elsewhere before attaching

options.name: -m
options.description: Force creation of a new session (ignore $STY)

options.name: -A
options.description: Adapt window sizes to current terminal

options.name: -L
options.description: Enable output logging to screenlog.N files

options.name: -T
options.description: Set terminal type for the session

args.name: session
args.description: Session name or PID to reattach to

options.name: -d
options.description: Detach the session first if attached elsewhere

options.name: -D
options.description: Detach and logout remote session before reattaching

args.name: session
args.description: Session name or PID to detach

options.name: -R
options.description: Reattach if possible, otherwise start new session

options.name: -RR
options.description: Use the first session if multiple detached

subcommands.name: -ls
subcommands.description: List all screen sessions and their states

options.name: -S
options.description: Filter listed sessions by name prefix

subcommands.name: -x
subcommands.description: Multiattach - attach to an already attached session (shared screen)

args.name: session
args.description: Session name or PID to multi-attach to

options.name: -r
options.description: Reattach if session is detached instead

subcommands.name: -X
subcommands.description: Send a command to a running session

args.name: command
args.description: Screen command to execute (e.g., quit, stuff, select)

options.name: -Q
options.description: Send command and wait for query response

globalOptions.name: -R
globalOptions.description: Reattach or create session if none exists

globalOptions.name: -X
globalOptions.description: Send a command to a named session

globalOptions.name: -c
globalOptions.description: Use specified screenrc file instead of default

globalOptions.name: -A
globalOptions.description: Adapt all windows to current terminal size

globalOptions.name: -Q
globalOptions.description: Query a running session command and return result

globalOptions.name: -wipe
globalOptions.description: Remove dead sessions instead of listing them

examples.description: Start a new named session called 'mysession'
Send quit command to a running session
Start a detached session running a script
Type 'ls' and Enter into a running session
Force detach from elsewhere and reattach here
